title: F6: self-describe fab_get_knowledge_sections — describe field names the 4 section enum values
description: The fab_get_knowledge_sections tool description currently says 'sections: pick one or more section names' but never lists the four enum values (MISSION_STATEMENT / REFERENCE / DIRECTIVES / EVIDENCE). LLMs without the schema in context guess wrong values. Rewrite the description to be self-contained: enumerate all four section names with a one-line semantic gloss for each, plus a usage example showing common combos.

files.change: Expand description text to list MISSION_STATEMENT (intent/what), REFERENCE (citations/links), DIRECTIVES (rules/must-do), EVIDENCE (examples/snippets); add one-line typical-combo hint

implementation: Read current tool descriptor and locate the `sections` field describe text
Compose the self-contained description listing the four enum values with concise glosses
Confirm enum is still source-of-truth: import the section enum from schemas and ensure description references the same set
Update any snapshot tests that pin the tool description string
Regenerate MCP manifest if cached (run `fab --list-tools` to spot-check)

reference.pattern: Self-describing MCP tool descriptors elsewhere in tools/
reference.examples: Mirror the tone of fab_plan_context's existing description

criteria: fab_get_knowledge_sections description string contains the literal substrings MISSION_STATEMENT, REFERENCE, DIRECTIVES, EVIDENCE
Snapshot tests, if any, are updated and pass

rationale.chosen_approach: Plain description rewrite — no schema change needed since the enum is already authoritative
alternatives_considered: Add per-section sub-tools (rejected: over-engineering for 4 enum values)
rationale.tradeoffs: Slightly longer description text (still under budget)

manual_checks: List tools via `fab --list-tools` and grep the four section names in the printed description
success_metrics: LLM call-success rate proxy: agent picks correct section name without schema injection
